channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: London Offices Without AC Become ‘Extremely’ Hard to Lease
item.description: Demand for London offices without AC was already lower than for buildings that had it as of 2024, according to property consultancy Knight Frank. Offices with no AC “will prove extremely challenging to let” as London temperatures continue to rise, says Dominic Pozzoni, head of national offices at Colliers. Related: Scientists Affirm Climate Change Fuels Extreme Weather There’s no recent public data on how widely air conditioning has been adopted across London’s offices.

item.title: Driving Above The Speed Limit Costs You — And Doesn’t Actually Save Much Time
item.description: They found that if drivers of light-duty, conventional internal combustion engine vehicles actually drove at posted speed limits, it could save an average of $22 million, based on fuel costs at the time, 6.7 million gallons of fuel and 57,000 metric tonnes of carbon dioxide every day. More than 43% of the studied trips included at least one instance of speeding, and drivers spent nearly 12% of their driving time going faster than the speed limit. If your objective is to get to your destination safely and to save fuel, then you might drive slower than the speed limit,” said William Northrop, mechanical engineering professor at the University of Minnesota and study co-author.

item.title: Lloyd’s coverholder Salt hires Chris Dent as marine manager
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Howden bolsters Japan natural resources team with Junichiro Ito as new head
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Strong 2025 insurance profits mask long-term structural challenges: Bain & Company
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Canada’s regulator adds catastrophe bonds as a form of reinsurance for capital credit
item.description: The regulator is changing Section 4.3 of the Minimum Capital Test Guideline, to include the use of natural catastrophe bonds as a form of reinsurance for capital credit, providing guidance for insurers that want to utilise cat bonds within their reinsurance towers. The Canadian financial markets regulator, the Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions (OSFI), has now added natural catastrophe bonds to its rules on allowable forms of reinsurance that can reduce capital requirements for insurance risk. Back in 2018, Canada’s Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions (OSFI) had been assessing the use of alternative reinsurance arrangements , including ILS and catastrophe bonds, cautioning at the time on the potential risks associated with third-party capital.

item.title: WTW names Coomey as Senior Director and Head of Exposure Management for ICT
item.description: Global insurance and reinsurance broker WTW has appointed Lisa Coomey as Senior Director and Head of Exposure Management within its Insurance Consulting and Technology business. In her new role, Coomey will support insurers in strengthening exposure management capabilities and applying catastrophe risk insights to strategic decision-making, portfolio management, business transformation, and emerging risk challenges. “Having worked across both industry and regulation, I look forward to building on the team’s strong foundation in Exposure Management and helping clients navigate an increasingly complex risk landscape through practical solutions, market insight, and deep industry experience.”

item.title: July European SCS outbreak to potentially cause losses over a billion euros: Aon
item.description: Widespread severe convective storms (SCS) across Europe between July 10–17, 2026, will likely result in substantial economic and insured losses, with aggregate financial losses expected to reach at least hundreds of millions of euros and the potential for the sequence to become a billion-euro industry event as damage assessments continue, according to Aon’s recent Weekly Cat Report. According to the event details, between July 10-17, a strong ridge of high pressure dominated northwestern Europe between the British Isles and Scandinavia, trapping a moist, hot air mass over Western and Central Europe. In the report, Aon explained that the damage was primarily driven by hail, with additional impacts from strong winds and localised flash flooding, with most losses concentrated in France and Germany.

item.title: MSIG Europe appoints new leaders to drive growth in France and Italy
item.description: MSIG Europe, the European insurance platform of MSIG Insurance Europe AG and part of the global MS&AD Insurance Group, has announced changes to its senior leadership team in France and Italy. MSIG Europe said his experience in developing businesses, managing change and building effective teams will support the company’s ambitions in France. In his new position, he will be responsible for leading MSIG Europe’s French operations, with priorities including strengthening client and broker relationships, supporting sustainable profitable growth and continuing to enhance the company’s market presence through specialist expertise and service.

item.title: Texas Heat to Challenge State Grid’s Power Demand Record
item.description: A blast of intense heat across eastern Texas is set to challenge the state grid’s all-time power demand record. Preliminary estimates from the Electric Reliability Council of Texas, or Ercot, show demand late Monday afternoon neared the 85.508 gigawatts record set in August 2023. An Ercot spokesperson said the power demand record was not eclipsed on Monday, according to official meter readings.

item.title: People Moves: MacLean Named VP, Head of Surety at Frankenmuth
item.description: Frankenmuth Insurance promoted Keith MacLean to vice president, head of surety. MacLean has over 20 years of industry experience and most recently served as vice president, commercial surety field operations. In his new role, MacLean will be responsible for setting the division’s strategic direction, driving profitable growth, strengthening agency and broker relationships, and fostering a culture focused on underwriting excellence and exceptional service.

item.title: Niubiz suma nuevas líneas de negocio para capitalizar la infraestructura Tapp
item.description: A pocos meses de arrancar las operaciones de la plataforma de pagos del Banco Central de Reserva del Perú (BCRP), empresas locales preparan sus posiciones para participar y monetizar esta infraestructura. Niubiz, adquirente peruano, adelantó a iupana dos aristas comerciales que desplegarán una vez que la plataforma opere: la iniciación de pagos y el switch bancario. “Estamos buscando ser un iniciador de pagos para asegurar que nuestra plataforma actual no pierda competitividad, pero de otra estamos buscando jugar un rol de enable r para el ecosistema”, dijo a iupana Gustavo Leaño, CEO de Niubiz.

item.title: WTW Launches Mortality Model for PRT Market
item.description: (Image credit: IIR/Gemini.) WTW (London) has launched a new version of its Geospatial Mortality Model for the U.S. The model has been trained on nearly four million life-years of mortality data, including post-COVID experience through 2024, according to WTW. “We are thrilled to partner with our colleagues in Insurance Consulting and Technology to expand the reach of WTW’s Geospatial Mortality Model,” comments Beth Ashmore, Senior Managing Director, Retirement, WTW.

item.title: Cyclosporiasis Cases in Michigan Climb Above 6,000, State Health Officials Say
item.description: Michigan health officials reported 6,148 cases linked to an outbreak of cyclosporiasis on Monday, an increase of 1,146 cases since their last update on Friday. The outbreak in Michigan has resulted in 102 hospitalizations, according to the latest update from the Michigan Department of Health and Human Services. No deaths have been reported in the Michigan outbreak.

item.title: Snap Nears Settlement of Addiction Case Ahead of Jury Trial
item.description: The first case went to trial in February in California state court and a jury found Meta and Google liable for a 20-year-old woman’s mental health struggles, awarding her a total of $6 million in damages. said it has reached a tentative agreement to settle the second case set for trial over claims that the world’s largest social media platforms are addictive to minors, leaving only Meta Platforms Inc. The terms of Snap’s tentative settlement were not disclosed Monday.

item.title: Insurer Interest in AI Exclusions Growing as Risk Becomes Omnipresent
item.description: Three ISO exclusions have garnered more interest from carriers, according to an attorney who practices in the cyber space and an expert who helped write the exclusions. “Insurers are still evaluating their options in real time, so we don’t know yet what each insurer is going to do in regard to AI exclusions or how broad their exclusions are going to be, or necessarily what lines of coverage are going to contain these exclusions,” McMullin said. However, he added, any new exclusions they introduce tend to garner a lot of interest.

item.title: Trade credit insurance seen as vital shield as global cash collection cycle stalls at ‘high plateau’
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: New York City Braces for Heavy Rain Tuesday After Weekend Floods
item.description: Flooding rain is set to make a return to New York and New Jersey Tuesday, days after a weekend storm blocked city streets and forced pedestrians to wade through thigh-high water in some areas. Up to four inches (10 centimeters) could fall Tuesday as storms pass over the New York City area during the morning and evening hours, said Joe Pollina, a meteorologist with the National Weather Service. New York’s emergency management department has warned that conditions may be similar to those that paralyzed parts of the city on Saturday.

item.title: Becoming a Frontier Insurer
item.description: Explore how Frontier Insurers use AI, GenAI, and Agentic AI to lead on competitiveness, cost structure, and growth in the intelligent era. The center of gravity is shifting from "AI as a data tool" to "AI as a workforce multiplier," powered by the Frontier Firm—companies built on on-demand intelligence and human-agent teams, where staff act as "agent bosses." Leaders are already scaling GenAI and Agentic AI with mature data capabilities behind them; laggards risk losing ground on performance and cost. AI has moved past the hype stage—it's reshaping cost structures, competitiveness, and growth across insurance.

item.title: FDA Walks Back Positive Lab Test While Lettuce Recall Continues
item.description: The FDA had said Saturday that it had found a positive test linking cyclospora to Taylor Farms’ lettuce that wasn’t part of the current recall, suggesting it could widen. Taylor Farms said the “FDA has not identified a single positive product test result for cyclospora.” Health officials are using other methods to trace the outbreak back to Taylor Farms produce, given the short shelf-life of lettuce and the specific challenges of testing for cyclospora. The Food and Drug Administration on Sunday withdrew an earlier positive test that had tied Taylor Farms’ lettuce to the cyclospora outbreak, even as health officials said the supplier remains a focus of their investigation.

item.title: Gulf Coast Warned of Flash Flooding as Storm System Looms
item.description: A slow-moving storm system lumbering across the northern Gulf of Mexico is threatening to bring as much as 6 inches (15 centimeters) of rain this week, prompting officials to warn of flash flooding from Florida to Texas. Tropical storm watches now stretch from the Florida panhandle to the Mississippi border as the system gradually heads northwest and sweeps across the Gulf Coast. Last month’s short-lived Tropical Storm Arthur skirted the Texas coast, bringing heavy rains to the region.

item.title: Insurance Woes Amid US-Iran War Stall Airlines’ Return to Dubai
item.description: and Iran escalate are slowing the return of some foreign airlines to Dubai, the chief executive of the entity that owns and manages its airports said. International carriers’ operations to Dubai are growing but some key European airlines still aren’t making the trip because of government travel advisories for the region, according to Dubai Airports Chief Executive Officer Paul Griffiths. Emirates, which flew near-empty planes to its hub in Dubai at some points as travelers sought to avoid the Persian Gulf, recently started providing war travel insurance to passengers.

item.title: U.S. Judge Will Allow Meta to Lay Off Workers Despite AI Discrimination Lawsuit
item.description: District Judge William Orrick in Oakland, California, in a written order said he would not stop Meta from carrying out the layoffs beginning July 22 while the merits of the workers’ novel legal claims are decided in private arbitration. judge on Friday rejected a bid by 26 employees of Meta Platforms to block the tech giant from laying them off while they pursue claims that they were targeted for job cuts by the company’s AI-powered tools because they have disabilities or took medical leave. The judge said the workers could not show that losing their jobs amounted to the “irreparable harm” required for him to issue an emergency order blocking the layoffs.

item.title: California Theft Crew Busted, Responsible for $1.3M in Stolen Vehicles
item.description: Members of an organized vehicle theft ring responsible for targeting luxury and high-performance vehicles throughout Southern California have been arrested, according to an announcement last week by the Ventura County Sheriff’s Office East County Special Enforcement Unit. During the course of the investigation, detectives relied heavily on the expertise of the Ventura County Auto Theft Task Force, whose investigators possess extensive experience in identifying stolen vehicles, fraudulent registrations, cloned vehicle identification numbers (VINs), fictitious license plates, and other methods commonly used by organized auto theft crews to conceal stolen property. On July 1, 2026, detectives from the East County Special Enforcement Unit in partnership with the Ventura County Auto Theft Task Force, Ventura County Organized Retail Theft Task Force, East County Investigations Bureau – Property Crimes Unit, and the Camarillo Special Enforcement Unit conducted a coordinated enforcement operation in Los Angeles County resulting in the arrests of two individuals on charges including Vehicle Theft, Conspiracy, and Possession of Stolen Vehicles.

item.title: Significant ILW interest at mid-year renewals. Very active cat bond market expected in Q4: SCOR IP
item.description: At the same time, after a record opening half to the year, catastrophe bond activity is expected to be very active in Q4, a new commentary from SCOR Investment Partners has highlighted. Reflecting on this year’s June and July renewals, SCOR Investment Partners noted that there was significant interest in purchasing ILW protection against US hurricane and US earthquake. “According to market expectations, Q4 should see a very active primary market, which could lead the market to break last year’s full year record of USD 25 billion,” SCOR Investment Partners said.

item.title: As severe thunderstorm plays larger role in ILS, exposure growth & claim severity trends are key: PCS’ White
item.description: While the re/insurance market continues to soften and both aggregate and multi-peril structures return to many catastrophe bonds, Harry White of Property Claim Services (PCS), a unit of Verisk, notes that severe thunderstorm losses remain a critical factor. White revealed that Verisk loss data from 1998 to 2025 shows that average annual severe thunderstorm insured losses in the United States have increased from approximately $4 billion (1998-2005) to over $40 billion (2021-2025), a significant jump. The executive also highlighted that the data points towards two key drivers: a national increase in insured exposure across severe thunderstorm-exposed states, and a sustained rise in the average cost of claims.

item.title: RMA, Inc. appoints MacKenzie as President, Myles to retire
item.description: RMA, Inc., a member company of Starwind Specialty, a large programme management operations firm in North America, has appointed Rob MacKenzie as President, effective July 6th, 2026. In the new role, MacKenzie succeeds Claire Myles, who transitions into an advisory role to support a seamless leadership transition, following a distinguished career spanning three decades. Under MacKenzie’s leadership, RMA will continue to focus on sustainable growth, innovation, and pursuing opportunities in emerging and underserved markets.

item.title: Chubb Tempest Re names Wixtead as Executive Chairman and O’Donnell as President
item.description: Global property and casualty insurer Chubb has appointed James Wixtead as Executive Chairman of its global reinsurance business, Chubb Tempest Re, and Michael O’Donnell as Senior Vice President, Chubb Group, and President of Chubb Tempest Re, succeeding Wixtead. As President of Chubb Tempest Re, O’Donnell will assume day-to-day executive management responsibility for the business, including both top and bottom line. Greenberg, Chubb Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, and John Keogh, President and Chief Operating Officer of Chubb Group.

item.title: Clear Group’s revenue climbs 44% to £134m in 2025
item.description: Clear Group has revealed its financial results for the year ended 31 October 2025, delivering strong financial and operational performance driven by the continued execution of its long-term growth strategy. Notably, Clear’s revenue rose 44% year on year to £134 million, from £93 million. According to Clear, growth was supported by expansion into European and North American markets across specialist lines of business, while adjusted EBITDA increased by 130%, reflecting both revenue growth and improved operational leverage.

item.title: Former Italian Motorway Boss Among 32 Convicted Over 2018’s Genoa Bridge Tragedy
item.description: T he former head of Italian motorway operator Autostrade per l’Italia was sentenced to 12 years in prison on Thursday [July 16] for his role in a disaster that killed 43 people when a bridge collapsed near the city of Genoa in 2018. Giovanni Castellucci was among 32 people convicted over the collapse of the Morandi bridge which sent vehicles plunging onto warehouses and a riverbed beneath the flyover during a summer storm. Michele Donferri Mitelli, another former senior Autostrade manager, got 11 years.

item.title: Nominate Your Agency as a Best Agency to Work For
item.description: If so, nominate your agency for Insurance Journal’s 2026 Best Agency to Work For awards today. Does your retail insurance agency stand out as a great place to work? Once again, Insurance Journal is seeking the best retail insurance agencies to recognize for the annual Best Agencies to Work For awards.

item.title: Aon Ups Data Center Lifecycle Program Capacity Again: Now $5B
item.description: Aon plc is expanding its proprietary Data Center Lifecycle Insurance Program (DCLP) with an additional $1.5 billion, increasing the program’s first-party coverage capacity to $5.0 billion. “As clients build larger and more complex data center portfolios, they need access to greater insurance capacity alongside solutions that strengthen resilience throughout the asset lifecycle.” The program builds on previous enhancements that increased capacity to $3.5 billion. Related articles : Data Center Boom Offers Organic Growth Opportunities for Brokers Like Aon, Marsh ; Deep Dive: Understanding Data Center Perils Launched in July 2025 , Aon’s Data Center Lifecycle Insurance Program is a multi-line insurance solution designed to address the interconnected construction, operational, cyber and financial risks facing data center owners, developers and investors.

item.title: Corgi’s $2.6 Billion Valuation Draws Attention After Rapid Funding Step-Up
item.description: Insurtech startup Corgi , which provides business insurance for startups, has raised $106 million in a Series B1 funding round at a $2.6 billion valuation , just three weeks after announcing a $160 million Series B that valued the company at **$1.3 billion. The rapid doubling of the company’s valuation has attracted attention within the venture capital industry, particularly because several investors participated in both funding rounds, prompting discussion around the use of so-called “internal markups.” Valuation doubles in three weeks The latest funding follows a busy fundraising period for the company. Investors cite business growth Corgi’s investors maintain that the higher valuation reflects the company’s underlying performance rather than financial engineering.

item.title: Prudential Singapore launches indexed universal life plan for legacy planning
item.description: Prudential Singapore has introduced PRUApex Legacy Index II , a new indexed universal life (IUL) insurance plan designed to give affluent and high-net-worth individuals greater flexibility in growing, protecting and transferring wealth across generations. Executive comment Toni Fung, Chief Customer and Marketing Officer at Prudential Singapore, said customers are increasingly seeking legacy planning solutions that can adapt to changing financial priorities. index with uncapped returns) in an indexed universal life plan two years ago.

item.title: Generali launches €300 million investment programme to support European SMEs
item.description: “In our role as a responsible insurer and investor, Generali can mobilise long-term capital in support of projects that concretely contribute to Europe’s resilience, competitiveness and autonomy.” Luca Cetrano, Group Chief Investment Officer of Generali, said the programme is intended to improve access to long-term financing for companies, SMEs and infrastructure projects operating in strategically important sectors. Generali has launched a €300 million European Sovereignty Program aimed at improving access to long-term financing for small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), strategic infrastructure projects and key industrial sectors across Europe. Building on previous investment initiatives The European Sovereignty Program follows Generali’s  Fenice 190  initiative, launched in 2021, which mobilised  €3.5 billion  to support Europe’s post-pandemic economic recovery.

item.title: Insurance AI Needs a Policy Preview Layer
item.description: My current role requires me to analyze and deliver a process workflow to preview and validate policy documents. Yet, there are always auditors and governed policies in insurance and banking institutions for every policy document, or every autonomous decision — any deviation from expected outcomes can trigger regulatory scrutiny. My intent was to develop an end-to-end policy documents ecosystem with the ability to AI-preview and AI-validate every incremental change before hitting the "go" button.

item.title: Cosign establishes insurance agency
item.description: Rental guarantor platform Cosign has established an insurance agency as it looks to expand beyond its core lease-guarantee offering. Cosign Insurance Agency received a resident insurance producer license in New Jersey on June 17, 2026 , covering property, casualty and surplus lines. The agency also secured general lines and surplus lines licenses in Texas on July 13.

item.title: Renaissance launches program for emerging independent agencies
item.description: Renaissance Alliance has launched Renaissance Launch, a program providing start-up and emerging independent insurance agencies with carrier access, technology, operational infrastructure, and strategic support. The program is designed for captive agents transitioning to the independent channel, licensed professionals starting agencies, small agencies looking to scale, and commercial-focused firms seeking broader market access. This program is designed to make that transition more practical by providing access to markets and operational support from the outset.” – David Gonzalez, Director of Emerging Markets, Renaissance.

item.title: Coventry pushes to make Abacus documents public
item.description: “They contain evidence of fraudulent business practices that go to the heart of this case.” Coventry argues that the documents relate directly to public representations Abacus made about its operations and to the issues Abacus placed before the court by filing its lawsuit. The legal battle between Abacus Global Management and Coventry First has moved to a fight over what the public gets to see. The court denied Coventry’s first motion to seal, finding that reliance on the parties’ confidentiality agreement was not enough to justify withholding the documents from the public.

item.title: SBS: «No quisimos poner un camisón de fuerza», por qué el regulador peruano evitó una regla única para comunicar incidentes
item.description: Una nueva norma peruana opta por no establecer una receta única para comunicar incidentes de seguridad, bajo el criterio de que cada entidad financiera debe adaptar estos protocolos a su realidad operativa, en lugar de seguir un esquema uniforme para todo el sistema. Accede al análisis que impulsa la toma de decisiones en la banca latinoamericana Este contenido exclusivo está disponible solo para miembros registrados de iupana. Regístrate gratis o inicia sesión para leer el artículo completo y descubrir: Perspectivas estratégicas sobre innovación, regulación y tendencias en banca digital y fintech Entrevistas con líderes que están transformando el sector financiero en la región Reportes y estudios que anticipan los cambios clave del mercado Acceso prioritario a información para tomar decisiones informadas y fortalecer tu liderazgo Únete a la comunidad de ejecutivos que marcan la diferencia en la transformación digital de la banca.

item.title: AM Best confirma los ratings de las principales entidades operativas de GCO
item.description: AM Best ha ratificado en ‘A’ (Excelente) la nota de fortaleza financiera de Occident y en ‘a+’ (Excelente) la calificación crediticia de emisor a largo plazo, con perspectiva estable en ambos casos.

item.title: Unión Alcoyana amplía su seguro de decesos con nuevas coberturas
item.description: Unión Alcoyana Seguros ha actualizado su seguro de decesos con la incorporación de nuevas garantías opcionales, entre las que destacan una cobertura de ayuda a la dependencia y una indemnización por el fallecimiento del perro, con el objetivo de ampliar la protección más allá del servicio funerario tradicional.

item.title: Los nichos que anticipan el futuro del seguro
item.description: Durante años, el desarrollo del negocio asegurador se ha apoyado en una lógica bien conocida: relaciones estables, pólizas recurrentes y clientes vinculados a largo plazo. Sin embargo, en paralelo —casi de forma silenciosa— han ido emergiendo soluciones que responden a un patrón distinto de comportamiento. Son los llamados seguros especializados o seguros de nicho.

item.title: Executives on the Move at SiriusPoint, Canopius Group, Badger Mutual
item.description: Canopius Group appointed Nadine Moore as chief executive officer of its U.S. She will report to Group CEO Neil Robertson and will join the group leadership team to deliver on Canopius’ long-term strategy in the region. Badger Mutual Insurance Company has named Shane Paltzer as director of strategic growth.

item.title: Beyond the Weather News Bulletins: How Insurers Can Improve NatCat Responses
item.description: When a wildfire, hurricane, tornado or flood is unfolding, the news media plays a critical role in alerting local populations. News media coverage that is built to inform the public doesn’t translate into actionable information a claims team can use to understand which of its insured locations sits inside the footprint. But in the decade my colleagues and I have spent working through these events alongside carriers, I have seen how little of that coverage actually helps the claims executive who has to respond.

item.title: Aon appoints Joe Liang as executive chairman of reinsurance for greater China
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Marsh Risk bolsters Asia marine team with trio appointment
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Prosper Capital LLP enters creditors’ voluntary liquidation
item.description: On 1 June 2026, Prosper Capital LLP (Prosper) went into creditors’ voluntary liquidation. Its designated members took steps to place Prosper into creditors’ voluntary liquidation. Prosper was also responsible for the activities of its appointed representative, Crowd2Let Capital Limited (Crowd2Let) (FRN: 743371).

item.title: Mexico Extends Its Venture Lead Over Brazil As More Global VCs Enter Latin America
item.description: For the third quarter in the past year, Mexico-based companies raised more venture capital in Q2 than their Brazilian counterparts, Crunchbase data on startup funding in Latin America shows, as Silicon Valley investors including Founders Fund and Andreessen Horowitz (a16z) led some of the largest deals in the region. (We expect the Q2 deal count to rise somewhat over time, however, as seed rounds in particular are commonly reported weeks or months after they close.) There were five nine-figure raises in Latin America in the second quarter, and as mentioned earlier, three of those were by Mexico City-based companies. Startups in Latin America raised a combined $1.36 billion across seed- and growth-stage deals in the second quarter, up 47% year over year and 22% from the first quarter.

item.title: Near-miss for Mexico’s parametric catastrophe bond on Friday’s M7.3 earthquake
item.description: After a magnitude 7.3 earthquake struck just off the southern coast of Mexico on Friday, analysis suggests this event was a near-miss for attaching the parametric trigger of one tranche of notes from the country’s World Bank facilitated IBRD CAR Mexico 2024 catastrophe bond transaction. Mexico has a robust natural disaster risk transfer and insurance program in place, at the heart of which sits its World Bank supported catastrophe bond issuances, as well as its traditional parametric insurance program. The Government of Mexico had secured $420 million of parametric disaster insurance protection against earthquakes and Atlantic hurricanes through the World Bank facilitated IBRD CAR Mexico 2024 catastrophe bond transaction, issued in early April 2024.
